items.title: Canberra water catchment shows signs of climate change

children.content: Like melting glaciers in Antarctica or bleached coral on the Great Barrier Reef, Namadgi National Park is showing signs of damage linked to global warming.

gallery.altText: Views of a river in a national park

thumbnail.altText: Views of a river in a national park

items.title: Canberra man pleads guilty to performing Nazi salutes in public

children.content: A Canberra man has pleaded guilty after performing Nazi salutes at two suburban shopping centres and placing white supremacist stickers over Indigenous and multicultural signs at the Australian National University.

image.altText: A young man leaves court.

items.title: Jo Clay elected as leader of ACT Greens, Andrew Braddock named deputy

children.content: Jo Clay is elected leader of the ACT Greens in a party vote, with Andrew Braddock as deputy leader, ushering in a new era after Shane Rattenbury's 14 years at the helm.

image.altText: A woman with dark hair and a green blouse speaks, standing outside near a green hedge.

items.title: Comedian says 'terrifying' roadside assistance encounter has easy solution

children.content: Comedian Laura Johnston calls for changes to the law after saying she was confronted by an NRMA contractor who could not provide identification, behaved aggressively and swore at her before leaving her stranded in remote NSW.

gallery.altText: A woman with dark hair and glasses looking into the camera, speaking.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with dark hair and glasses looking into the camera, speaking.

avatar.alt: Niki Burnside is a journalist and digital producer based in Canberra.

items.title: Teen charged twice in three days over alleged stolen car pursuits

children.content: A 14-year-old boy charged twice in three days after allegedly leading police on stolen car pursuits is among several incidents prompting the federal police union to call for tougher bail laws in the ACT.

image.altText: A screenshot of police dash-cam footage showing officers making an arrest beside the road.

items.title: Three new bulk-billing GP clinics opening in Canberra

children.content: Three new bulk-billing GP clinics are opening in Canberra, but questions remain about how they will be funded and staffed long-term.

image.altText: Doctor tools hanging on the wall of a medical consultation room

items.title: Police say 'miracle' no-one killed as six trucks destroyed in fiery crash

children.content: An investigation is underway after six semi-trailers were destroyed by fire when trucks crashed into a rest area south-west of Goulburn.

image.altText: A fireball caused by a burning truck in the dark.

gallery.altText: rfs truck with blaze behind it on highway road

thumbnail.altText: rfs truck with blaze behind it on highway road

items.title: Man who stabbed wife in front of daughter found guilty of attempted murder

children.content: An ACT Supreme Court jury has found a Canberra man guilty of attempted murder after he stabbed his wife during a violent attack in the family's Gungahlin home.

image.altText: The ACT Supreme Court towards dusk.

items.title: Does Pauline Hanson's claim of growing support in the ACT pass the pub test?

children.content: The conventional wisdom is that Canberra is a largely left-leaning city, but the national capital is now among places in One Nation's sights as support for the party surges across the country.

image.altText: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson answers a question following her address at the National Press Club

items.title: 'One of the greatest Antarctic collections in the world' on display in Canberra

children.content: For most, a trip to Antarctica is well beyond reach, but a new exhibition at the National Museum of Australia gives visitors the chance to explore life in one of the most extreme environments on Earth.

items.title: Micro 'cockroach' drones help find earthquake survivors in Venezuela

children.content: A week after devastating twin earthquakes struck Venezuela rescue teams are using drones, dogs, cameras and specialised listening equipment to search for survivors.

articles.title: Satellite images show Venezuelan earthquakes wiped out entire towns

children.content: Venezuela's devastating earthquakes flattened buildings and coated parts of the northern coast in dust and debris, satellite imagery shows.

image.altText: An aerial image of rows of residential homes and an ocean-side boat marina with significant earthquake damage.

items.title: Catholic Schools NSW chief to stand aside amid corruption probe

children.content: The head of the body governing the state's catholic schools will stand aside after he was named in a corruption probe investigating donations to the NSW Liberal Party.

image.altText: Man in dark suit at media microhones

items.title: MP's company fined $555k over worker's quad bike death

children.content: The court heard the 82-year-old worker was not wearing a helmet and the quad had no rollover protection when the crash occurred.

image.altText: A woman in a blue shirt holding a hat standing in front of some sheep.

items.title: Pictures show interior of Trump's new Air Force One jet

children.content: The jet, gifted to the US government by Qatar has been retrofitted with Trump shaking off concerns over the cost.

items.title: Australia ranks third-highest for median wealth globally, UBS data shows

children.content: Australia holds the third-highest median wealth globally at $306,074, pipped at the post by Luxembourg and Belgium.

image.altText: Office workers cross the street in Sydney

items.title: Why F1's rumour mill is speculating futures of Piastri and Verstappen

children.content: F1's rumour mill has been in overdrive in recent times about Oscar Piastri's future at McLaren, with his boss having to hose down suggestions the Australian will be driving anything other than a Papaya-coloured F1 car in 2027.

image.altText: Oscar Piastri and Max Verstappen sitting next to each other during the drivers' photo

items.title: Bonus ATAR points not enough to fix Australia's Asian language 'fruit salad'

children.content: A parliamentary report recommends a sweeping overhaul of Asian language education in Australia, saying the lack of knowledge is a sovereign risk to the country.

image.altText: Two girls sit next to each other smiling at the camera

items.title: As they mourn their daughter, family wants a ‘life for a life’ from accused man

children.content: The family of a Thai teenager allegedly killed by an Australian man has placed her cremated ashes to rest at a local Buddhist temple as they seek justice for her murder.

image.altText: A thai woman in  a beige shirt holds a white photo frame.

items.title: Nobody knows where John Frum came from but he is god in this village

children.content: On Vanuatu's Tanna Island, I immerse myself in a culture that worships a shape-shifting spirit known as John Frum.

gallery.altText: Kate dances with a woman in a field in Vanuatu.

thumbnail.altText: Kate dances with a woman in a field in Vanuatu.

items.title: Jackass began with a gunshot. Now it's a $500m franchise

children.content: For more than 25 years, prank franchise Jackass has been delighting teens and terrifying parents. Now the gang is heading to the cinemas for what could be their last ride.

image.altText: A bull gores a man dressed as a matador

items.title: Matthew Flinders’s steamy letters reveal a hidden side to the man who named Australia

children.content: Flinders circumnavigated and named Australia. Impressive stuff, but he also wrote many impassioned letters and lived a life full of crushes.

image.altText: A letter from 1800, cursive ink writing and faded paper

items.title: Quick hits: Feuding Belgians save the day, England claims 'disadvantage'

children.content: Belgium stars turn on each other before combining to save their team, England makes claims of "disadvantage", and Congo's goalkeeper gives it all he's got. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

articles.title: USA's World Cup dream alive despite red card against Bosnia and Herzegovina

children.content: The US seals progression to the last 16 with a 2-0 win over Bosnia and Herzegovina despite losing a man to a red card, while Belgium converts a penalty in extra-time to beat Senegal 3-2.

image.altText: Folarin Balogun points to his forehead in celebration

articles.title: When do the Socceroos play next and how to watch the knockout match

children.content: Australia's journey at the 2026 World Cup continues this weekend as the Socceroos take on Egypt. Here's everything you need to know.

image.altText: Players line up and sing their national anthem.

articles.title: Mile Jedinak knows what it's like to take a World Cup penalty

children.content: Now an assistant coach for the Socceroos, and with a penalty shootout a possibility when Australia face Egypt in the round of 32, Mile Jedinak knows what it takes to seize the moment.

children.content: The federal government says a group of foreign nationals has been removed from Australia after reaching Far North Queensland illegally by boat this week.

items.title: Russia strikes in central Kyiv kill at least eight people and injure dozens

children.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy cut his Dublin trip short as the strikes trapped paramedics and drivers at an ambulance station.

image.altText: A woman walks past a burnt out car.

items.title: Former St Mary's Hostel site recognised as key Stolen Generations place

children.content: Former residents say the permanent heritage listing of the St Mary's Hostel site in Alice Springs is an important step towards healing, truth-telling and remembering the children taken there under assimilation-era policies.

items.title: 'Less water than one restaurant': AI data centre company responds to concerns

children.content: One of three AI factories planned for northern Tasmania would be quieter than a library to the nearest resident, Firmus co-CEO Oliver Curtis says, as the company moves to assuage community concerns around power use, water and noise.

items.title: ASX edges down while shoe retailer Betts shuts stores

children.content: The ASX edges down following a sea of red on Wall Street. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: 'Have to get me off at gunpoint': The fight for people's right to live on boats

children.content: For Australians on the brink of homelessness, dilapidated boats have become a way to stave off sleeping on the streets, but many are being removed from these vessels amid a safety crackdown.

items.title: World's biggest porn site puts advertising block on Australian AI empire

children.content: Australian pornography website OurDream has had its advertising suspended by Pornhub after the ABC revealed OurDream's content could include child exploitation material.

image.altText: A montage of profile photos of characters with blurred faces and age estiamtes beside them.

items.title: Are parents reconsidering letting kids play footy over CTE risks?

children.content: How is the conversation about lasting brain disease from repeated head knocks on the sporting field impacting parents' decisions about what sports their children play?

image.altText: A close shot of paint on the grass at Brookvale Oval.

items.title: 'At greatest risk of CTE': Expert calls out dangers of heading the ball

children.content: With all eyes on the World Cup, Australian football's management of the risks associated with the practice of heading the ball, such as dementia and CTE, is in the spotlight.

thumbnail.altText: A soccer player in blue and orange heads a ball in front of trees in a park.

items.title: Lethal Weapon actor Danny Glover says he has Alzheimer's

children.content: Danny Glover, who turns 80 in July,  has revealed that he has Alzheimer's disease and is leaning on his family for support as he lives with the incurable condition.

image.altText: Danny Glover is in a black suit and patterned bowtie, standing by a red wall with the word Oscars written on it.

items.title: Police rescue 'feral' children from squalor inside dilapidated US home

children.content: Sixteen children from the same family rescued from a dilapidated rural home in Ohio were living in wretched conditions with human waste all around, authorities say.

image.altText: A white, weatherboard home with a grey tin roof and green lawns surrounded by yellow police tape with a pile of rubbish.

items.title: NSW sergeant 'snuck a look' for ex-cop mate accused of domestic violence

children.content: A NSW Police officer has admitted to unlawfully accessing the force's internal database to locate details about a domestic violence complaint against his friend.

items.title: Adelaide gets 70 per cent of July rain in one day, farmer has 228.5 reasons to smile

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology says metropolitan Adelaide has received 70 per cent of its median rainfall for July in a single day, as South Australian farmers welcome the conditions.

image.altText: water overflow from a grass patch over a short wall to a busy road
